title: Dynamic Asset Allocation with Asset-Specific Regime Forecasts
why_it_matters: Shows that regime information should be asset-specific, not only market-wide; combines regime forecasting with mean-variance allocation over 1991-2023 multi-asset data.
design_implication: Add asset-specific regime labels/features and test them inside the optimizer rather than only as one global regime flag.

title: Downside Risk Reduction Using Regime-Switching Signals: A Statistical Jump Model Approach
why_it_matters: Emphasizes online regime selection, trading delays, transaction costs, and downside-risk control.
design_implication: Use walk-forward regime inference and report downside metrics, not just returns and Sharpe.

title: DSPO: An End-to-End Framework for Direct Sorted Portfolio Construction
why_it_matters: Studies thousands of tradable stocks and avoids small curated universes.
design_implication: Scale from curated baskets toward large, reconstituted universes and separate small-basket evidence from broad-universe evidence.

category: stock selection over large equity universe
source: Research in International Business and Finance
title: S&P 500 Stock Selection Using Machine Learning Classifiers: A Look Into the Changing Role of Factors
why_it_matters: Shows feature importance changes over time and that frequent retraining matters.
design_implication: Track time-varying factor/feature importance and refresh screening models on a rolling basis.

title: A Meta-Analysis of Supervised and Unsupervised Machine Learning Algorithms and Their Application to Active Portfolio Management
why_it_matters: Uses a two-stage pipeline: stock selection first, optimization second, with market-phase comparisons.
design_implication: Split the project into explicit screening and weighting layers; compare across pre-stress, stress, and post-stress windows.

title: Portfolio Optimization by Enhanced LinUCB
why_it_matters: Illustrates context-aware controller selection in changing market conditions across multiple markets.
design_implication: Prototype a contextual bandit meta-controller that selects controller families based on universe/regime descriptors.

title: Hierarchical Reinforced Trader: A Bi-Level Approach for Optimizing Stock Selection and Execution
why_it_matters: Uses a high-level stock selector and low-level execution controller.
design_implication: If RL is used, use it hierarchically: high-level selection/routing first, direct low-level allocation only if justified.

title: A Review of Reinforcement Learning in Financial Applications
why_it_matters: Highlights benchmarking problems, robustness issues, contextual RL, multi-agent RL, model-based RL, offline RL, and risk-sensitive RL.
design_implication: Treat RL as one component in a rigorous benchmark stack with explicit baselines, frictions, and ablations.

title: Open Source Cross-Sectional Asset Pricing
why_it_matters: Provides broad stock-level predictor constructions and a reproducibility benchmark for large-universe studies.
design_implication: Use open replicated characteristics or their logic to define richer stock-level universes and screening layers.

title: Machine Learning and the Implementable Efficient Frontier
why_it_matters: Pushes evaluation toward net-of-cost implementability rather than only forecast accuracy.
design_implication: Make cost-aware efficient-frontier comparisons central to the next study, not an appendix.

title: The Term Structure of Machine Learning Alpha
why_it_matters: Shows that some ML alpha disappears after costs or at short horizons.
design_implication: Add explicit negative-result and cost-sensitivity sections to avoid overstating complex methods.
